page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores using the 64-bit ARMv9 architecture, specifically the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance and memory subsystem capabilities. The instance achieved a top-tier rating in multi-core Geekbench testing, software compilation with Clang, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with large-block read and write operations reaching top-tier classifications of 402.56 GB/sec and 198.42 GB/sec respectively. Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput also registered in the top tier. Conversely, single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, align with average tier results. Large language model text generation performance varies, showing weak throughput for small 135M models but strong throughput for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gn.12xlarge offers high resource density for multi-threaded workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading, as each of the 48 vCPUs maps to a dedicated physical core. The high network bandwidth of 150 Gbps combined with top-tier memory bandwidth provides a balanced profile for network-bound and memory-intensive compute tasks. The instance is suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving. It is less suited for workloads requiring high single-core performance or local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores on the 64-bit ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 96.0 GB of memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Network performance is supported by a 150 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation speeds, and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database operations and static web serving throughput. However, single-core performance and small-model text generation remain average to weak. This instance is suited for multi-threaded computing, software compilation, network-heavy applications, and database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 48 dedicated Graviton physical cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.12xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 48 dedicated cores, 96 GB RAM, and 150 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 48 dedicated physical cores, high-speed memory bandwidth, and 150 Gbps baseline network performance.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated physical ARMv9 cores running at 2.8 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier multi-core performance and memory read/write bandwidth
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
